could_apply_equally_to.reasoning: 2.47 is the mature statement of the doctrine 2.38 seeds. The engineering layer for 2.38 explicitly names the foreshadowing of 2.47 (right is to action, not to fruits) — but the 2.38 signature is the THREE-PAIR structure (sukha/duḥkha, lābha/alābha, jaya/ajaya) which 2.47 does not have. 2.47 is action-without-grasping; 2.38 is the dispositional precondition.

2.48 is the imperative form ('yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi'). The 'arrive identical' phrasing in 2.38's engineering layer is the dispositional content of yoga-sthaḥ but is held to the THREE-PAIR enumeration which 2.48 does not have. The two verses are intentionally cousins; 2.38 is the seed-statement, 2.48 is the imperative.

2.56 (the sthita-prajña whose mind is not disturbed in the midst of the three sufferings) shares the equanimity theme but is the description of the achieved state, not the seed-statement of the discipline.

verdict_reason: The engineering layer is anchored to the THREE-PAIR structural signature of 2.38 (Datadog/target/competitor as three distinct registers, identical disposition across all three) which is the verse's specific content. The 2.47 / 2.48 / 2.56 family is honored as cousins, not collapsed.
